name: 28 Days TWS
extraInfo: Both Pro and base PS5 run at a very stable 60fps and don't support HDR. While Pro renders at a slightly higher resolution, the difference in overall image quality is minimal. Shadows do however exhibit less flicker and certain assets (like workbenches) have more image stability on Pro.

name: ActionCam: SC
extraInfo: Both Pro and base PS5 run at a very stable 60fps. However, you can significantly increase the FOV, and it's here where Pro will have an easier time maintaining 60fps in more demanding scenes. Pro potentially has a slightly higher resolution than base PS5, but any difference is negligible - especially when the camera effects are toggled on.

name: Only Up Rush
extraInfo: Both Pro and base PS5 run at a very stable 60fps. However, Pro has a slightly higher resolution, resulting in a cleaner/more 4K-like image, with less shimmer/instability in assets like railings/billboards/tarp.

name: Road Cafe Simulator
disclaimer: All tech information is based off Motel Business Simulator as there's no public information about Road Cafe Simulator's Pro enhancements. However, as both games are by Nostra, in the same genre, and have a similar aesthetic/scope, MBS provides a good baseline of what to expect from RCS:
extraInfo: 'Both the Pro and base PS5 version are the same for all intents and purposes. For example, the framerate is a locked 60fps, with no HDR support, and there's excessive shimmer on both consoles. At best, Pro has a negligible increase in resolution that you'd find hard to notice even when looking at both versions side by side. Non-Pro enhanced games like Metaphor: ReFantazio showcase more significant improvements simply due to Pro's increased horsepower.'
